issue.name: SQL injection
issue.issueBackground:
SQL injection vulnerabilities arise when user-controllable data is incorporated into database SQL queries in an unsafe manner.

See the reference.
issue.remediationBackground: Use parameterized queries (prepared statements) for all database access.
issue.issueDetail: The category parameter appears to be vulnerable to SQL injection. The payload ' OR '1'='1 returned additional rows.

issue.name: Cross-site scripting (reflected)
issue.issueBackground: Reflected XSS arises when user input is echoed into the response without sanitisation.
issue.remediationBackground: HTML-encode user input on output and apply a Content Security Policy.
issue.issueDetail: The q parameter reflects unencoded input.

issue.name: Cross-site scripting (reflected)
issue.issueBackground: Reflected XSS arises when user input is echoed into the response without sanitisation.
issue.remediationBackground: HTML-encode user input on output and apply a Content Security Policy.
issue.issueDetail: The comment parameter reflects unencoded input.

issue.name: Cookie without HttpOnly flag set
issue.issueBackground: If a cookie is not flagged HttpOnly it can be read by client-side scripts.
issue.remediationBackground: Set the HttpOnly attribute on all session cookies.
issue.issueDetail: The session cookie does not have the HttpOnly flag.

issue.name: Information disclosure - server banner
issue.issueBackground: The server discloses version information in response headers.
